qa.question: What is a consistent element in John's self-identification throughout the conversation?

C. He believes he is a caveman.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: I called myself John. I almost always do.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: As tales of the resurrection spread, the name was confused with the Hebrew "Yochanan", meaning "God is gracious". My stay on earth was seen as divine proof of immortality. That led to "God is salvation", or Hebrew "Yahshua", which in translation became my proper name, changing to late Greek, "Iesous", Then to late Latin, "Iesus", and finally medieval Latin, "Jesus".

reasoning_steps.inference: In D18:64, John explicitly states he calls himself John.
In D18:65, he explains how that name evolved into Jesus, reinforcing that his original name was John.
Thus, consistently he identifies as John.

qa.question: Which statement best describes Dan's approach to John's story?
option: A. He is skeptical and dismissive.
B. He is curious and open to possibilities, while providing historical context.
C. He is emotionally invested and tearful.
D. He is indifferent and bored.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: We think that language came into existence sixty thousand years ago. The structure of Stone Age culture is evidence of the ability to communicate... verbally.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Look, we have two simple choices. We can get all bent out of shape intellectualizing or bench- pressing logic, or we can simply relax and enjoy it. I can listen critically, but I don't have to make up my mind about anything.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: That is fascinating, though, isn't it, a brave attempt to teach Buddhism in the West.

reasoning_steps.inference: Dan provides historical context in D16:12.
He advocates for relaxing and enjoying the story in D18:4, showing openness.
He finds it fascinating in D18:1, showing curiosity.

evidence_dialogues.utterance: Since I graduated Harvard medical school and finished my residency, I've had that feeling.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Will
evidence_dialogues.utterance: I had a private practice for a while, and then I taught.

reasoning_steps.inference: In D15:7, Will mentions graduating from Harvard medical school and residency, indicating medical training.
In D15:10, he mentions having a private practice, which is typical for a psychiatrist.
Thus, he is a psychiatrist.

qa.question: In which two instances does Will express amusement or exasperation?
option: A. When John says he is as sane as the caveman, and when Edith insists John wasn't Jesus
B. When Harry jokes, and when Dan asks a question
C. When John talks about fire, and when Sandy smiles
D. When Art mentions Sacre du Printemps, and when Linda asks about medicine men
E. When Dan says 'our caveman', and when Harry says 'dream on'

reasoning_steps.inference: In D15:16, Will laughs after John says 'I think you're as sane as he is.'
In D18:50, Will says 'Oh, Edith' in response to Edith's outburst, showing exasperation.
These are two instances of amusement/exasperation.

qa.question: What is the chronological order of Will's career events as he describes them?
option: A. Graduated Harvard, had private practice, taught, met caveman
B. Had private practice, taught, graduated Harvard, met caveman
C. Taught, had private practice, met caveman, graduated Harvard
D. Met caveman, graduated Harvard, had private practice, taught
E. Graduated Harvard, taught, had private practice, met caveman

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Will
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Since I graduated Harvard medical school and finished my residency, I've had that feeling.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Will
evidence_dialogues.utterance: I had a private practice for a while, and then I taught. Nothing unusual- Oh, until one day, I met a caveman who thought he was Jesus.

reasoning_steps.inference: In D15:7, he mentions graduating first.
In D15:10, he says he had a private practice, then taught, then met the caveman.
Thus the order is: graduated, private practice, taught, met caveman.

qa.question: What physical actions does Will perform in scene 1a and scene 2?
option: A. He lies on the couch and then rises to his feet
B. He stands by the fire and then sits down
C. He exits the room and then returns
D. He warms himself and then leaves
E. He plonks himself and then walks out

reasoning_steps.inference: In D15:3, Will lies on the couch.
In D18:51, he rises to his feet.
These are his physical actions in the two scenes.

qa.question: Which two characters does Will directly address in the conversation?
option: A. John and Edith
B. Dan and Harry
C. Sandy and Linda
D. Art and John
E. Edith and Dan

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Will
evidence_dialogues.utterance: When did you begin to believe you were Jesus?

reasoning_steps.inference: In D15:5, Will addresses John directly.
In D18:50, Will addresses Edith directly.
Thus he directly addresses John and Edith.

session_1.text: Scene 1a: Eventide
Fade in, Will is warming himself in front of the fire, it is darker.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: From the Buddha to the cross, I have always imagined both as entirely mythic. Ye- but I would like to hear more.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: May I lie on the couch for a moment? I'm not as young as I used to be.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: (plonks himself on the couch)

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Ohh! Hah, so! You were Jesus. Well, perhaps somebody had to be, for better or for worse. The jury is still out.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: When did you begin to believe you were Jesus?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: When did you begin to believe you were a psychiatrist?

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Since I graduated Harvard medical school and finished my residency, I've had that feeling.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Oh, I sometimes dream about it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Have you acted upon this belief?

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: I had a private practice for a while, and then I taught. Nothing unusual- Oh, until one day, I met a caveman who thought he was Jesus.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Do you find that unusual?

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Very. I would stake my reputation he is as sane as I am, so why does he persist in such a story?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: There must be a reason, though.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Unless I imagined it all? Is that possible?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I think you're as sane as he is.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Oh, god, I- (laughs)

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: No.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Did you ever find it prudent to worship yourself rather than be thought a heretic? That would be something!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: At the time Christianity was considered heresy, I had to pretend other faiths.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: And what does Jesus have to say to those present who find it difficult to believe in Him?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Believe in what he tried to teach, without rigmarole. Piety is not what the lessons bring to people. It's the mistake they bring to the lessons.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (slowly getting up and putting on his jacket)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Well, it's getting to be night. I still have stuff to carry and a long drive.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: I'll help.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: John, do you have a destination in mind?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Never mind. I won't ask.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Thank you.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (exits with Sandy, carrying boxes)
Scene 1b: Debate

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Anyone mentally ill can imagine a fantastic background- Even an entire life- and sincerely believe it. The man who thinks he is Napoleon does believe it. His true identity has taken a backseat to his delusion and the need for it. If that's the case with John, there is a grave disorder.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Organized brilliantly. He's got an answer for everything.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: It might involve rejection of his father, of his entire early past, replaced by this fantasy.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: He says he can't remember his father.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Precisely. Why?

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: You said he was sane.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Did I?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Do you think that perhaps our caveman has a monkey on his back?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Drugs?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: No, no, no, no. I've done a lot of consulting work with the narcotics division. I've seen people...tripping, strung out- Whatever's up with John, it isn't that. I've looked for signs- None.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Could cavemen really talk?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We think that language came into existence sixty thousand years ago. The structure of Stone Age culture is evidence of the ability to communicate... verbally.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Harry wolf whistles, clicks his tongue.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Oh, shut up.
Scene 1c: Sandy
John is looking at the stars.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Maybe it'd be easier if I were.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Crazy? No.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Coyote howls. John then looks at Sandy.
Scene 2: The Last Spell

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: That is fascinating, though, isn't it, a brave attempt to teach Buddhism in the West. It's no wonder he failed. We're not ready for it.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You're talking as if you believed him.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, it is possible, isn't it? I mean, anything is possible.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Look, we have two simple choices. We can get all bent out of shape intellectualizing or bench- pressing logic, or we can simply relax and enjoy it. I can listen critically, but I don't have to make up my mind about anything. But you think you do?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Well, unfortunately, there's no authorities on prehistory, so we couldn't stop him there.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: There are experts on the bible.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Chheh, dream on.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Yeah, thus the lost years of Jesus. He didn't exist until John put on a hat.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I don't believe in angels and the nativity and the star in the East, but there are stories about the childhood of Jesus.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: History hates a vacuum. Improvisation, some of it very sincere, fills the gaps. It would have been easy to falsify a past back then- A few words, credulity- Time would do the rest.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Now you're talking as if you believed him.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Well, look at the popular myths surrounding the Kennedy assassination in a few short years- You had, uh, conspiracy, mafia, CIA- That's a mystique that'll never go away.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's always been a small step from a fallen leader to a god.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I don't think anybody will deify Kennedy. We're more sophisticated than that.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We are?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We are.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: John and Sandy enter.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Well, you're finally fulfilling one prophecy about the millennium, John.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: What's that?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Here you are again.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: John makes himself comfortable between Sandy and the fireplace.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: You like the fire, John.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Everywhere I've lived, I've had a fireplace.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Childhood fixation, I guess. Helps me to feel secure.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: (smiling)

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: There are predators out there.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: One thing I didn't pack...I thought I might need it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: John puts on a tape/record of Beethoven's 7th Symphony.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Wouldn't Sacre du Printemps be more appropriate?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: What?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: You've got...four men of science completely baffled, my friend. We- We don't know what to make of you.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Did you know Voltaire was the first to suggest that the universe was created by a gigantic explosion? I think Paul would agree. And then Goethe was the first to suggest that spiral nebulae were swirling masses of stars. We now call them galaxies. It's kind of funny how often new concepts of science find their first tentative forms of expression in the arts.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: So did Beethoven do physics on the side?

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: He spent most of his time lying on the floor in front of his legless piano surrounded by orange peels and apple cores.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Now we're on the floor listening to Beethoven.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Full circle.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Did you have, um... any religious beliefs? Or didn't you give it much thought?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: You can't get there with thought.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: You have faith?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: In a lot of things.

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Do you have faith in the future of the race?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I've seen species come and go. Depends on their balance with the environment.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We've made a mess of it.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: There's still time, if we use it well.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Christianity has been a worldwide belief for 2,000 years.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: How long did the Egyptians worship Isis? Or the Sumerians Ishtar? In India, sacred cows wandered freely as reincarnated souls. In a thousand years, they'll be barbecued and their souls will be in squirrels.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (firmly)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You weren't Jesus!

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: John smiles.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Oh, Edith.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: (rises to his feet)

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: (clearing her throat)

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: If it rains, y-...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (interrupting)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: -It won't.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: How do you know that?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I don't smell it.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Were you... I guess a... medicine man?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I was a shaman a few times. I… revealed some truths to eat a little better.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You think that's all religion is about...

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: selling hope and survival?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: The Old Testament sells fear and guilt. The New Testament is a good code of ethics, put into my mouth by poets and philosophers that are much smarter than I am. The message is never practiced. Fairy tales build churches.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: What about the name "Jesus"? Did you pull that out of a hat?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I called myself John. I almost always do.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: As tales of the resurrection spread, the name was confused with the Hebrew "Yochanan", meaning "God is gracious". My stay on earth was seen as divine proof of immortality. That led to "God is salvation", or Hebrew "Yahshua", which in translation became my proper name, changing to late Greek, "Iesous", Then to late Latin, "Iesus", and finally medieval Latin, "Jesus".

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: And it was a wonder to watch it all happen.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Then you didn't claim to be the Son of God?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Began as a schoolhouse and ended as a temple. I said I had a master that was greater than myself. I never said he was my father. I wanted to teach what I learned. I never claimed to be king of the Jews, I never walked on water, I never raised the dead. I never spoke of divine except in the sense of human goodness on earth.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (pause)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: No wise men came from the East to worship at a manger. ...I did do a little healing with some eastern medicine I'd learned... That's it.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: The three wise men began as a myth about the birth of the Buddha.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: John, I should be home, uh, kissing my wife.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: We're all here, trapped by your story...hoping for a...revolution? I don't know.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Are there any, uh...more revelations for us?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: John shakes his head, smiling.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's just like old times.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (after a pause, glaring at John, and then firmly, again)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You. Weren't. Jesus.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Quote the Sermon on the Mount.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Which one? Darby? King James? New American Standard?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Do you know them all?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: No one knows the one, not even me.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I...I did some teaching on a hill one day. Not that many people stayed.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: But you...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Biblical Jesus said, "Who do you think I am?" He gave them a choice. I'm giving you one.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (timidly, her voice quivering, eyes tearing up)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: W-were you?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: If I said no, could you ever be sure?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Edith's lips quiver, and she suddenly lets out a sob. Art switches the light on to break the spell.
